metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1257
metadata.comment:
Rationale: The authors of the study generate tomosyn-2 KO mice and study their motor performance and synaptic activity at neuromuscular junctions. Geerts et al monitor increased in spontaneous acetylcholine release at diaphragm NMJs of KO animals by means of ex vivo electrophysiology (miniature endplate potentials, MEPPs). In addition, paired pulse facilitation is reduced at physiological stimulation rate (endplate potential decrease) (fig. 3). From these observations, the authors conclude that tomosyn-2 inhibits basal acetylcholine release and suggest that tomosyn inhibits vesicular release propensity, which is an intrinsic property of the vesicular fusion machinery.
However, further investigation is required in order to prove the molecular mechanisms by which tomosyn-2 inhibits vesicular fusion.. Experimental description: Materials and methods

Animals
A tomosyn-2 knock-in targeting vector (Tom2lox) was
constructed by flanking exon 2 (Genbank accession number
AY542329) with loxP sites (online resource supplementary
Fig. 1). Homologously targeted Tom2lox embryonic stem
cells derived from a C57Bl/6 background were injected
into wild-type blastocysts and implanted into pseudopreg-
nant females. Chimers were mated with inbred C57Bl/6
mice (Caliper Life Sciences). Tom2KO mice were obtained
by mating C57Bl/6 Tom2lox mice with a C57Bl/6 CMV
Cre deleter strain (gift from R.J. Pasterkamp) recombining
the loxP sites.
